# Medical Education

Research and news on artificial intelligence in medical education.

Items are selected nightly by an automated pipeline and link to their original sources. See [About](../about.md) for how selection works.

<img class="section-banner" src="../../assets/section-medical-education.svg" alt="">

<div class="section-brief">
<p>A rapid scoping review highlights expanding artificial intelligence integration across healthcare professional degree programs in the United States, underscoring a shift in curricula that incorporates AI-driven tools and learning strategies <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42370260?fc=20260609215449&amp;ff=20260629131807&amp;v=2.20.0">[3]</a>. Research demonstrates that AI implementation in clinical skills education has accelerated since 2022, introducing new approaches for simulation and assessment <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42359442?fc=20260609215449&amp;ff=20260626113417&amp;v=2.20.0">[14]</a>. Studies continue to assess knowledge, utilization, and perceptions of AI among medical students and faculty, revealing variability in experience and competency with generative models in educational contexts <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42367557?fc=20260609215449&amp;ff=20260629131807&amp;v=2.20.0">[5]</a> <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42367454?fc=20260609215449&amp;ff=20260629131807&amp;v=2.20.0">[6]</a>. Additionally, systematic reviews indicate large language models are being increasingly adopted to deliver automated feedback in medical imaging education, supporting instructional innovation <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42359091?fc=20260609215449&amp;ff=20260626113417&amp;v=2.20.0">[15]</a>.</p>
<p class="section-brief-date">The picture as of June 30, 2026; numbered links go to the items below.</p>
</div>
